mcqs.question: A user types 'www.google.com' in a browser. What is the primary role of the DNS in this scenario?

options.text: It encrypts the data exchanged between the browser and the web server
It translates the human-readable domain name into an IP address
It establishes a persistent connection to the web server
It caches all HTTP responses from the web server

mcqs.explanation: DNS serves as a distributed database that maps domain names to their corresponding IP addresses, enabling browsers to locate servers on the network.

mcqs.question: Which HTTP method is idempotent and used to retrieve a representation of a resource without causing any side effects on the server?

options.text: POST
PUT
GET
DELETE

mcqs.explanation: The GET method requests a representation of the specified resource and should only read data without modifying server state.

mcqs.question: A client sends a request to download a file from a server. The server responds with HTTP status code 204. What does this indicate?

options.text: The requested resource has been permanently moved to a new URL
The request succeeded but there is no content to send in the response body
The client must authenticate itself to get the requested response
The server encountered an unexpected condition preventing it from fulfilling the request

mcqs.explanation: HTTP 204 No Content indicates success with no message body, commonly used in AJAX scenarios where the client doesn't need to navigate away.

mcqs.question: An organization changes the IP address of their web server. If the TTL value for their DNS record is set to 3600 seconds, what is the maximum time before all recursive resolvers will have the updated IP address?

options.text: Less than 1 second since the change was made
Exactly 3600 seconds after the change
Up to 3600 seconds, depending on when each resolver last queried the record
The TTL has no effect on propagation time

mcqs.explanation: TTL determines how long resolvers cache the DNS record; until cache expires, they may serve stale data showing the old IP address.

mcqs.question: A web application needs to distinguish between a first-time visitor and a returning user. Which HTTP header field is most appropriate for maintaining session state?

options.text: User-Agent
Authorization
Cookie
Content-Type

mcqs.explanation: The Cookie header allows the server to store and retrieve session data on the client side, identifying returning users.

mcqs.question: What two primary security benefits does HTTPS provide over HTTP?

options.text: Faster page loading and compression of data
Encryption of data in transit and server authentication via certificates
Prevention of all types of malware and phishing attacks
Anonymous browsing and unlimited caching

mcqs.explanation: HTTPS uses TLS/SSL to encrypt communication and provides server authentication through digital certificates, preventing eavesdropping and impersonation.

mcqs.question: Compared to HTTP/2, what is the key advantage of HTTP/3 in terms of connection establishment?

options.text: HTTP/3 uses TCP and requires a three-way handshake for each request
HTTP/3 uses QUIC over UDP, eliminating the head-of-line blocking issue and reducing connection latency
HTTP/3 does not support header compression
HTTP/3 is not compatible with TLS encryption

mcqs.explanation: HTTP/3 builds on QUIC (which uses UDP) to reduce connection setup latency and eliminate head-of-line blocking between streams.

mcqs.question: DNSSEC is designed to protect against which type of attack in the DNS infrastructure?

options.text: DDoS attacks that overwhelm DNS servers with traffic
DNS cache poisoning where attackers inject false DNS records
Man-in-the-middle attacks on HTTP sessions
SQL injection attacks on DNS management databases

mcqs.explanation: DNSSEC adds cryptographic signatures to DNS records, allowing clients to verify their authenticity and detect tampered responses.
